title: tragacantha meridionalis
intro: Tragacantha meridionalis, also known as the Mediterranean milk thistle, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, known for its distinctive purple flowers and spiny leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly the seeds, which contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The silymarin in Tragacantha meridionalis has been found to have health benefits, including protecting the liver from damage, reducing inflammation, and improving overall well-being. Tragacantha meridionalis can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, which are used to support liver health and treat various liver-related conditions.
